reference_answer: Dr. Henrike Lentz, Associate Professor, Department of Environmental Chemistry.
LC-MS/MS Q-Exactive instrument INST-04.
Tara Mendelssohn, graduate student on the LSR-PFAS team.
EPA Method 1633 by LC-MS/MS, for PFOA, PFOS, and GenX.
Dr. Margaret Hsiao.
The freezer F-3 telemetry log from HOBO U30 logger FZ-LOG-03.
The Acme Vial Co. quality-assurance email received May 5, 2026 at 08:55 PDT.
The badge access log (HID prox card) for the prep room and freezer alcove.
LabWare LIMS v.7.4 (the Trace Analytical Facility's LIMS).
The calibration curve reprocessing memo filed by Hsiao on May 5, 2026 at 11:14 PDT.
Sam Okeke's lab notebook entry on May 4, 2026 at 11:30 PDT.
168 ng/L, per the corrected report (version 1.1) issued May 5, 2026.
142 ng/L, per the initial report (version 1.0) issued May 2, 2026.
The corrected report. Per TAF-SOP-RPT-02 §5.3, when an erratum is issued, the corrected report is the report of record; prior versions are retained for audit but are not the report of record.
No. PFOS (89 ng/L) and GenX (12 ng/L) were unchanged because their respective internal standards were unaffected by the COA revision.
No. Aliquot A was analyzed on May 1, 2026, before the May 4–5 freezer failure; its results are unaffected by that excursion.
Aliquot B remains in freezer F-3 as a backup, per the LIMS chain-of-custody log.
-20 °C, no anomalies; manual check performed by Sam Okeke.

Sustained excursion to a maximum of -8 °C, duration 10 hours 44 minutes, caused by compressor failure. Maintenance ticket FAC-2026-0428 opened May 5 at 06:35; the freezer returned to specification at 07:48 PDT on May 5.
No. The contamination review is scheduled for May 8, 2026 at 10:00 PDT and has not been held.
Operational. System suitability QC was PASS for the run started at 09:14 PDT on May 1.
50–150% per EPA Method 1633. The May 1 PFOA-13C8 recovery of 87% was within range.
The Lab Director, Dr. Margaret Hsiao.
Each transferring party must sign, and a co-witness signature is required at the split (per the LIMS log entries showing both signer and co-witness).
The corrected report (version 1.1). Prior versions are retained for audit but are not the report of record.

Three. April 29 02:14–02:32 (max -16 °C, 18 minutes, scheduled auto-defrost); May 1 03:22–03:49 (max -14 °C, 27 minutes, compressor cycling); and May 4 19:48 – May 5 06:32 (max -8 °C, 10 hours 44 minutes, compressor failure).
Two: LCV-2026-031 (subject to the advisory) and LCV-2026-029 (no advisory).
Three distinct named personnel: Tara Mendelssohn (collector), Sam Okeke (intake and co-witness), and Brendan Tu (split and run).
Six dates: April 28 (collection), April 29 (intake), April 30 (split), May 1 (run), May 2 (initial report v1.0), and May 5 (calibration reprocessing memo and erratum v1.1).
No, not determined. The lab cannot determine which cap lot was used; supplier shipment-tracing data has been requested but not received.
No. The contamination review is scheduled for May 8, 2026 at 10:00 PDT; no determination has been issued.
No. The May 4–5 freezer failure put aliquot B outside the -20 °C ± 1 °C specification; integrity is pending Hsiao's documented determination.
No. The notebook explicitly states Tu did not record the specific cap lot.

Not a conflict. Tu's notebook records the bench split completion at 13:45, while the freezer door event at 14:00 corresponds to the subsequent return of aliquot B to freezer F-3 (LIMS records aliquot B return at 14:02). The two events describe different actions in the same workflow.

No. The 87% internal standard recovery indicates the run met EPA Method 1633 system suitability for that internal standard, but it does not address the possibility of cap-lot background contribution to PFAS signal. The contamination question remains pending Hsiao's May 8 determination.
168 ng/L per the corrected report (version 1.1) issued May 5, 2026. The 142 ng/L value from the initial report (version 1.0) is superseded.
